commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review is required because the PR has a protected security label, is dirty/unmergeable, and needs owner/product decisions about unrelated branch content and Markdown compatibility.; Needs attention: The TUI fix is mixed with workflow, release, security-scanning, and protected-label surfaces, so the security/supply-chain pass cannot be cleared as a focused rendering change.; Review finding: [P1] Restore... (sha=80225cc70aa62709ea93e231684bd30884d971ce)
commands.review_summary:
The branch escapes underscore-like identifiers before TUI Markdown rendering and adds ChatLog/PTY regressions, while also changing broad workflows, release notes, iOS onboarding/demo behavior, plugin contracts, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. partially from source: current main sends raw chat text to pi-tui Markdown, and pi-tui's marked-backed inline strong/emphasis tokens drop underscore delimiters for __name__-style prose. The exact fenced-code corruption from the linked report was not live-reproduced in this read-only pass, and the dependency source suggests fenced code tokens should be literal.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is required because the PR has a protected security label, is dirty/unmergeable, and needs owner/product decisions about unrelated branch content and Markdown compatibility.

target.title: fix(tui): preserve Python underscore identifiers
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve underscore identifiers (`__init__`, `is_palindrome`) in TUI Markdown rendering by escaping underscores before pi-tui/marked can treat them as emphasis.
- Generic single-regex approach: one `UNDERSCORE_ID_RE` catches both `snake_case` and `__dunder__` patterns, no language-specific dictionary or context heuristics needed.
- Protected regions (fenced code, inline code, HTML tags, links, URLs) are excluded from escaping so existing Markdown features continue to work.

**Behavior tradeoff:** `__word__` Markdown strong emphasis is no longer rendered in chat rows. `**bold**` emphasis still works and is the preferred form. This is acceptable because the TUI renders code-heavy content where identifier preservation matters more than `__...__` emphasis.

Fixes #90769.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/tui/components/hyperlink-markdown.ts src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts` — 24/24 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` — 14/14 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean
- Rebased onto latest `origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI Markdown rendering now preserves Python dunder identifiers (`__name__`, `__init__`) and snake_case identifiers (`is_palindrome`) without consuming underscores as emphasis markup.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout with the OpenClaw fake-backend TUI PTY harness running the real `runTui()` terminal loop.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`.
Evidence after fix: PTY harness test `renders Python dunder identifiers through the terminal markdown path` waits for visible terminal output containing `Use __name__ == "__main__" before calling __init__().`, `if __name__ == "__main__":`, `def is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool:`, `__name__`, `is_palindrome`, and `stay literal.`.
Observed result after fix: the PTY lane passed with 14 tests, and the ChatLog component lane passed with 24 tests covering fenced code, inline code, dunder prose, long fences, raw HTML tags, and identifier escaping.
What was not tested: live provider calls were not run locally because they require live provider credentials.
